landmark: TH (RD>TR) Trailhead with vault toilet near Upper Canjilon Lakes campground.

name: S3 52.2 - Toilet
landmark: W2 2017. Campground proper (E) reportedly also has faucets and vault toilets, likely seasonal.

name: S4 52.9 - Toilet
landmark: to backtrack up to the trailhead at previous waypoint and then follow Crossing (W3) roads south to County Rd B007 / Dunn Bridge crossing of the Rio Grande, 20 miles total, then continue 2 mi farther to the village of Arroyo Hondo at NM Hwy 522. End of trail from Sheep Crossing of Rio Grande. Toilet and table here (no water). (CCW if you find the Rio too dangerous to ford, then you may need to backtrack to Cerro or Questa and take an RTD bus around. Bus stops in village of Arroyo Hondo, then hike or hitch down to familiar

name: S5 8.0 - Toilet
landmark: TH (RD>TR) reference on signs. Modern vault toilet. Picnic table(s). Signed Latir Peak Wilderness boundary. Occasional access to Cabresto

name: S6/7 27.0 - Toilet
landmark: San Cristobal (PO L) to postmaster Mike) but ask permission first. Bathroom, water, and trash can also con permiso. A little ways back down the road, along the route, is the modest-looking (but not inexpensive) Taos Gogi Eco Lodge in a bucolic setting, with occasional rooms available on short notice. An RTD Blue Bus stop is also within reach, with daily runs to Taos south and Questa north. (c) Brett Tucker CCW = counter-clockwise (fall hike); JL / JR = junction, turn left or right (for CW travel along the route, reverse for CCW); XR = cross road; XT = cross trail; !! = take note SE CW CCW Shown on Mapset. Shading icon color. Carsonite post in large cairn at top of switchbacks. End of Goose Creek Tr 65 at nexus of trails. Somewhat confusing on map, and only the main route ahead (W) is really visible on the ground here above treeline. This S6 9.6 TJ (S6A START)

name: S7 16.8 - Toilet
landmark: !! XR (END XC) 64 into Rio Grande Gorge Bridge rest area. Your extended stint of xc travel ends here. West Rim TH / Gorge rest area. Developed water (drinking fountains) 24/7. Restrooms closed after 5pm. Shade ramadas. Didn’t see vending.

name: S7 28.8 - Toilet
landmark: Taos Jct bridge W3 Orilla Verde Campground as well as a boat launch / take-out with vault toilet. Picuris Trail TH. Good flowing spring at roadside (6-2020) is 0.25W.

name: S7 30.0 - Toilet
landmark: W1-2 Light spring flow across Slide Trail among willows 6-20 Upper trailhead for Slide Trail. Popular spot among mtb'ers using new trail network in area (not the Slide Trail so much). Vault toilet here. CW head E on the paved county road (110). You're finally out of the vast Rio

name: S7 30.7 - Toilet
landmark: TH JL (TR>RD) Grande del Norte National Monument for good, along with public lands between here and your upcoming town stop at Ranchos de Taos, so no camping allowed ahead. (CCW note that you want to join the Slide Trail here from the TH, which heads N just W of the toilet.) You don't want the road heading due N here, but rather the lesser utility

name: S8 23.3 - Toilet
landmark: Waypoint S8 23.3 N) on highway (parking and vault toilet across the road). Be careful with traffic on this narrow winding 2-lane road. (c) Brett Tucker CCW = counter-clockwise (fall hike); JL / JR = junction, turn left or right (for CW travel along the route, reverse for CCW); XR = cross road; XT = cross trail; !! = take note SE CW CCW Shown on Mapset. Shading icon color. It's a mere half mile off-route to the laid-back Sipapu Ski & Summer Resort. True to its name, Sipapu offers year-round services, namely a motel and cabins, restaurant / grill, and something of a convenience- store level grocery option. Sometimes busy, other times a virtual ghost town, Sipapu has agreed to hold hiker packages in the past but call Sipapu resort (M L ahead to confirm current arrangements to ensure a smooth resupply S8 36.15398, -105.54773 8193' maildrop) here. Information has historically been as follows: UPS deliveries only to Sipapu Ski & Summer Resort, Attn Lisa (Please Hold for -hiker's name here-), 5224 Hwy 518, Vadito NM 87579. Contingent upon overnight stay. CALL FIRST 800-587-2240 www.sipapunm.com Or just stop by in hopes of enjoying a quick burger and beer on the back patio built along the banks of the Rio Pueblo. S of paved NM 518, bridged crossing of Rio Pueblo along entrance road
